complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break down into subtasks covering: (1) React Native project initialization with TypeScript, (2) folder structure setup and dependency installation, (3) TypeScript/ESLint/Prettier configuration, (4) theme system with colors and typography, (5) React Navigation setup with bottom tabs, (6-7) font loading (Poppins/Quicksand), (8) React Query provider configuration, (9) Redux store with persistence, (10) base screen components with Paper theme, (11) Metro bundler optimization, (12) missing dependencies installation, (13) complete folder structure, (14) font integration, (15) TypeScript validation, (16) final iOS/Android launch testing. Note: Project is already partially complete with navigation, theme, and basic screens implemented.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Project already has significant progress - navigation with 5 tabs exists, theme system with colors/typography implemented, React Query and Redux configured in App.tsx, and screens are placeholder implementations. Remaining work includes font loading setup, Redux persistence, folder structure completion (components, services, utils, types, assets missing), and Metro optimization. Score 7 because while foundation exists, several critical pieces need completion including font loading, API client setup, and build optimization.

complexityAnalysis.taskTitle: Setup Node.js Backend with Express and PostgreSQL
complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break down into: (1) backend directory initialization and core dependencies installation (Express, TypeScript, Prisma, Redis, JWT, validation libraries), (2) TypeScript and Express app structure setup with routes/controllers/middleware/services folders, (3) PostgreSQL database configuration and Prisma initialization, (4) Prisma schema design with core entities (User, Restaurant, PointsTransaction, FoodiePoints, Order, SocialPost, Challenge, Event, Donation), (5) first database migration execution, (6) Redis connection setup for caching and sessions, (7) JWT authentication middleware implementation with access/refresh tokens, (8) rate limiting middleware (100 req/min), (9) global error handling middleware, (10) health check endpoint and CORS configuration for mobile origins.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Greenfield backend development requiring full stack setup from scratch. High complexity due to: multiple database entities with relationships, dual authentication system (JWT + refresh tokens), Redis integration for caching/sessions, Prisma ORM setup with migrations, comprehensive middleware stack (auth, rate limiting, error handling, CORS). Score 8 because it's foundational infrastructure requiring careful architecture decisions and integration of multiple technologies (PostgreSQL, Prisma, Redis, Express, JWT) with security considerations.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Divide into: Backend (1) auth routes setup (signup, login, refresh, logout, forgot/reset password), (2) password hashing with bcrypt (cost 12), (3) JWT access (15min) and refresh (7day) token generation, (4) refresh token HTTP-only cookie storage, (5) token refresh logic, (6) user profile endpoints (GET/PUT/DELETE /users/me), (7) password reset email flow. Mobile: (8) auth screens (Login, Signup, Forgot/Reset Password) with validation, (9) auth service with axios and token storage (AsyncStorage), (10) automatic token refresh interceptor, (11) biometric authentication (Face ID/Touch ID), (12) user profile screen with photo upload, (13) onboarding quiz for preferences, (14) Redux user state slice.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Complex full-stack authentication requiring coordination between backend and mobile. High complexity (9) due to: dual token system (access + refresh) with secure storage, biometric integration (platform-specific APIs), photo upload with camera/gallery, email-based password reset, HTTP-only cookies, automatic token refresh interceptors, onboarding flow with preference collection, and Redux state management. Security-critical component requiring careful implementation of bcrypt, JWT, secure storage, and CORS. Dependencies on Tasks 1 & 2 mean this must integrate with existing mobile foundation and new backend.

complexityAnalysis.taskTitle: Build Unified Loyalty Wallet Backend & API
complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break into: (1) Prisma schema extension (Restaurant, PointsWallet, PointsTransaction models with relationships), (2) wallet API endpoints (GET wallet, POST connect, POST sync, GET history, DELETE disconnect), (3) restaurant OAuth integration service for supported chains (Starbucks, Chipotle), (4) manual entry fallback for unsupported restaurants, (5) background sync job with Bull queue + Redis (4-hour intervals), (6) points normalization algorithm for equivalent value display, (7) expiration tracking background job (daily checks), (8) push notification system for expiring points (30/7/1 days), (9) points calculation service with restaurant formulas and bonus multipliers, (10) database seeding with 20+ restaurant chains and loyalty programs, (11) API integration testing with OAuth flows.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Very high complexity (9) due to: third-party OAuth integrations with multiple restaurant APIs, background job scheduling with Bull + Redis, complex business logic for points calculation with restaurant-specific formulas and multipliers, expiration tracking requiring daily jobs and push notifications, points normalization algorithm across different loyalty programs, and extensive database seeding. Requires understanding of external API limitations, polling strategies, OAuth security, job queue reliability, and push notification infrastructure. Dependencies on Tasks 2 & 3 for backend foundation and auth.

complexityAnalysis.taskTitle: Develop Mobile Wallet UI with Real-Time Sync
complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Organize into: (1) Wallet screen components (WalletHeader, RestaurantCard, AddRestaurantButton), (2) pull-to-refresh manual sync, (3) restaurant connection flow with search/autocomplete (200+ restaurants), (4) OAuth webview integration for supported chains (react-native-webview), (5) manual entry form for unsupported restaurants, (6) transaction history screen with filters and timeline view, (7) expiration alerts banner and notification center, (8) in-app notification handling with Firebase, (9) animations (confetti on points earned, shimmer loading, transitions), (10) background sync setup (iOS background-fetch, Android WorkManager), (11) haptic feedback, (12) offline caching for wallet data.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: High complexity (8) due to: OAuth webview integration with deep linking, background sync requiring platform-specific implementations (iOS vs Android), real-time push notifications with Firebase, complex animations (confetti, shimmer, transitions), restaurant search with autocomplete across 200+ items, offline-first architecture with local caching, transaction filtering and pagination, and haptic feedback. Requires coordination with backend OAuth flows, understanding of platform-specific background task limitations, and performance optimization for smooth 60fps animations. Dependencies on Tasks 3 & 4.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Divide into: (1) mobile camera dependencies (react-native-vision-camera, permissions, image resizer), (2) backend Google Vision API setup with service account credentials, (3) /api/v1/receipts/scan endpoint (multipart upload), (4) OCR extraction logic (restaurant name with fuzzy matching using fuse.js, total amount regex, date/time, line items), (5) duplicate detection using image hash and 30-day check, (6) auto-credit points logic based on restaurant formulas, (7) mobile scanning UI (camera preview, framing guide, capture with haptic), (8) loading state during OCR (2-3s), (9) review screen with editable fields, (10) confirmation with confetti animation, (11) AWS S3 integration for receipt storage (90-day retention), (12) receipt history screen, (13) edge case handling (low quality, unknown restaurant, duplicates).
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Very high complexity (9) due to: Google Cloud Vision API integration requiring service account setup and cost management, complex OCR text extraction with regex for currency/dates, fuzzy matching algorithm for restaurant identification (handling typos/variations), duplicate detection using image hashing, AWS S3 setup for blob storage with lifecycle policies, camera implementation with permissions and error handling, image optimization before upload, multi-step UI flow (capture → loading → review → confirm), and comprehensive edge case handling. Requires expertise in computer vision, pattern matching, cloud services, and mobile camera APIs. Dependencies on Tasks 4 & 5.

complexityAnalysis.taskTitle: Build QR Code Scanner for Instant Points
complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Structure as: (1) QR scanning library installation (react-native-vision-camera with barcode plugin or react-native-qrcode-scanner), (2) backend QR validation endpoint design with JSON format {restaurantId, transactionId, amount, timestamp, signature}, (3) HMAC signature generation/validation using shared secrets per restaurant, (4) validation logic (unique transactionId, 10-minute timestamp window), (5) auto-credit points immediately on valid scan, (6) mobile QRScanScreen with camera preview and framing guide, (7) success animation + haptic feedback, (8) duplicate QR code blocking, (9) restaurant QR generation admin tool (web dashboard), (10) scan history in wallet, (11) rate limiting (10 scans/user/day).
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Moderate-high complexity (7) due to: QR code signature verification using HMAC for security, real-time validation with time-based constraints (10min window), duplicate detection to prevent fraud, rate limiting to prevent abuse (10/day), and admin web dashboard for QR generation. Less complex than receipt OCR (no computer vision or fuzzy matching) but requires strong security focus with signature validation, timestamp checks, and fraud prevention. Dependencies on Tasks 4 & 5 for wallet integration.

complexityAnalysis.taskTitle: Create Discovery Feed with Personalized Recommendations
complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break into: (1) Prisma schema extension with MenuItem model, (2) /api/v1/feed/restaurants endpoint with pagination, (3) ranking algorithm implementation (user preferences 40%, proximity 25%, recency 15%, engagement 10%, diversity 10%), (4) proximity calculation using PostGIS or Haversine formula, (5) collaborative filtering implementation ('users who liked X also liked Y'), (6) /api/v1/feed/trending-dishes endpoint, (7) local business spotlight logic (restaurants <3 locations), (8) mobile FeedScreen with infinite scroll using FlashList, (9) RestaurantCard component with quick actions (Save, Share, View Menu, Order), (10) filters (cuisine, price, distance, dietary), (11) 'Not Interested' swipe action, (12) GPS location tracking with permission handling, (13) feed refresh on location change (>1 mile), (14) restaurant detail page, (15) cold start for new users, (16) feed caching with 10min TTL.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Very high complexity (9) due to: sophisticated recommendation algorithm with 5 weighted factors requiring data science expertise, collaborative filtering implementation requiring user similarity calculations, geospatial queries using PostGIS/Haversine for proximity ranking, infinite scroll performance optimization with FlashList, GPS tracking with battery considerations, cold start problem for new users, feed caching strategy, and complex filtering/sorting. Requires algorithm design, database optimization for geospatial queries, performance tuning for large datasets, and UX considerations for personalization quality. Dependencies on Tasks 3 & 4 for user data and restaurant integration.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Organize into: Backend (1) Prisma schema extension (SocialPost, Comment, Follow models), (2) /api/v1/posts endpoints (POST create, GET feed, POST like, POST comment, DELETE), (3) feed algorithm (70% followed users, 20% recommendations, 10% trending, engagement boosts), (4) /api/v1/users/:id/follow endpoint, (5) content moderation with profanity/hate speech detection. Mobile: (6) CreatePostScreen with multi-photo selection (5 photos max), (7) image editor with food-optimized filters (Vibrant, Warm, Crispy) using react-native-image-filter-kit, (8) crop/rotate/adjust tools (brightness, contrast, saturation), (9) restaurant tagging with GPS-based autocomplete, (10) caption input with 500 char limit, (11) dish tags autocomplete, (12) privacy settings, (13) FeedScreen with infinite scroll, (14) PostCard component with photo carousel, (15) like animation (heart pulse), (16) comment modal with nested replies, (17) user profile screen (posts grid, followers/following), (18) AWS S3 presigned URLs for direct upload, (19) image compression before upload, (20) follow system and suggested users.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Maximum complexity (10) due to: full social network implementation requiring post creation, engagement (likes/comments), feed algorithm, follow graph, content moderation AI, AWS S3 integration with presigned URLs, advanced image editing with filters and adjustments, multi-photo upload with compression, nested comment system, privacy controls, and user profiles. Massive scope combining backend feed algorithms, AWS infrastructure, mobile camera/gallery, image processing libraries, real-time engagement updates, and content moderation. Most complex task requiring social network expertise, cloud storage architecture, image processing, and sophisticated feed ranking. Dependencies on Tasks 3, 4, & 8.

complexityAnalysis.taskTitle: Implement In-App Ordering System with Stripe
complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Structure as: Backend (1) Prisma schema extension (Order model), (2) Stripe SDK installation and account setup (test + production keys), (3) /api/v1/orders endpoints (POST create, GET order details, GET history, PUT status), (4) Stripe PaymentIntent creation, (5) payment capture on order confirmation, (6) fee calculation (15% restaurant, 5% service, $2-5 delivery), (7) auto-credit points after completion with formulas/multipliers, (8) restaurant POS integration (Square, Toast, Clover APIs) or email/SMS fallback. Mobile: (9) MenuScreen with categorized items, (10) MenuItem component with dietary icons, (11) item detail modal with customizations and special instructions, (12) cart bottom sheet with quantities and checkout button, (13) checkout screen (delivery/pickup toggle, address selection, order summary with fees, payment method), (14) Stripe Payment Sheet integration (@stripe/stripe-react-native), (15) 3D Secure handling, (16) 'Use Points' toggle (100pts=$1, max 50% discount), (17) order confirmation screen, (18) OrderTrackingScreen with progress bar and estimated time, (19) real-time status updates (WebSocket or polling), (20) push notifications for status changes, (21) delivery integration (DoorDash API or restaurant drivers).
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Maximum complexity (10) due to: complete e-commerce implementation with Stripe Payment Sheet, 3D Secure authentication, POS system integrations with multiple providers (Square/Toast/Clover), complex fee calculations, points redemption logic with constraints, real-time order tracking requiring WebSockets or polling, delivery integration with third-party APIs, customizable menu items, cart management, address handling, and payment security. Requires payment processing expertise, POS API integration knowledge, real-time communication architecture, and comprehensive error handling for payment failures. Critical business functionality with high security requirements. Dependencies on Tasks 3, 4, & 5 for auth, points, and wallet.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Divide into: Backend (1) Prisma schema extension (Challenge, FoodiePoints, Achievement models), (2) /api/v1/challenges endpoints (GET active, POST accept, GET progress, GET leaderboard), (3) challenge tracking implementation (unique restaurant counter, diverse dish counter, cuisine exploration counter), (4) milestone rewards logic (1st/5th/10th restaurant bonuses), (5) restaurant-sponsored challenge creation via web dashboard, (6) background job to check challenge completions after transactions, (7) leaderboard calculation (local, national, friends). Mobile: (8) ChallengesScreen with tabs (Active, Completed, Leaderboard), (9) ChallengeCard with progress bars and rewards display, (10) achievement badge showcase on profile, (11) challenge completion animation (badge scale + confetti). Redemption: (12) Tango Card API integration for digital gift cards, (13) /api/v1/rewards/redeem endpoint, (14) redemption catalog (restaurants, retailers, Visa prepaid), (15) pricing tiers (1000pts=$10, 2500pts=$25, 5000pts=$50), (16) gift card email delivery + in-app wallet, (17) Foodie Points separate wallet section, (18) 12-month inactivity expiration logic.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: High complexity (8) due to: gamification system with multiple challenge types requiring distinct tracking logic, milestone-based rewards with progressive bonuses, leaderboard calculations across different scopes (local/national/friends), Tango Card API integration for gift card fulfillment, restaurant-sponsored challenge creation requiring web dashboard, background jobs for completion detection, points expiration tracking, and comprehensive redemption catalog. Requires game mechanics design, third-party gift card API integration, leaderboard performance optimization, and scheduled job reliability. Dependencies on Tasks 3, 4, & 10 for user data, points, and transaction tracking.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Structure as: Backend (1) Prisma Event model extension, (2) /api/v1/events endpoints (GET events with filters, GET event details, POST register, POST checkin, DELETE cancel), (3) restaurant dashboard endpoint for event creation (POST /api/v1/restaurants/events), (4) event approval workflow (auto for verified, manual for new). Mobile: (5) EventsScreen with Feed/Map/Calendar views, (6) EventCard component with photo, type badge, date/time, location, distance, registration count, (7) event detail screen with RSVP status and directions, (8) QR code generation for check-in per registration, (9) calendar integration (add to device calendar), (10) map view with event pins using Google Maps, (11) event check-in validation (restaurant staff scans user QR), (12) reminder notification scheduling (1 week, 1 day, 1 hour before), (13) restaurant web dashboard for event management (React app with create/edit/delete forms).
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Moderate-high complexity (7) due to: multi-view UI (feed/map/calendar) requiring different rendering strategies, Google Maps integration with custom pins and directions, QR code generation and validation for check-ins, local notification scheduling with multiple reminders, calendar integration with device APIs, restaurant web dashboard requiring separate React app, event filtering by type/date/distance, and geolocation for proximity calculations. Less complex than social features but requires maps expertise, notification scheduling, and web dashboard development. Dependencies on Tasks 3, 4, & 8 for auth, points, and location services.

complexityAnalysis.taskTitle: Implement Value Dashboard and Budget Tracking
complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break into: Backend (1) /api/v1/value endpoints (GET best-value, GET savings, GET cost-per-meal, PUT budget, GET budget tracking), (2) value score algorithm implementation (pointsEarned/cost + availableDiscounts/cost + qualityRating), (3) cost per meal calculation grouped by restaurant, (4) savings tracking (monthly/lifetime from points discounts + promotions), (5) budget tracking logic with 80% alert threshold, (6) category breakdown (breakfast/lunch/dinner). Mobile: (7) ValueScreen/tab with BestValueCard components, (8) SavingsSummary with monthly trend graph, (9) BudgetTracker with progress bar and category breakdown, (10) CostPerMealChart (bar chart/ranked list), (11) DecisionHelper questionnaire (craving, price range, distance, priority), (12) recommendation logic for top 3 suggestions, (13) ValueAlerts for bonus points/expiring points/flash sales. Email: (14) daily value digest cron job (10 AM), (15) SendGrid integration for email delivery, (16) digest content (best value restaurants, active promotions, expiring points, meal suggestions).
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Moderate-high complexity (7) due to: value scoring algorithm requiring formula design and testing, budget tracking with category breakdown and alerts, cost analytics requiring aggregation queries, trend visualization with graphs, decision helper requiring questionnaire logic and recommendation engine, daily email digest with cron scheduling, SendGrid integration, and real-time value alerts. Requires data analytics expertise, charting library integration (Victory Native or similar), email template design, and scheduled job management. Less complex than core features but substantial analytical and visualization work. Dependencies on Tasks 3, 4, & 10 for user data, points, and orders.

complexityAnalysis.taskTitle: Build Community Impact and Donation System
complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Organize into: Backend (1) Prisma schema extension (Charity, Donation, FoodDrive models), (2) /api/v1/impact endpoints (GET charities, POST donations, GET donation history, GET impact dashboard, GET leaderboard, GET food drives, POST participate, POST checkin), (3) donation processing (points conversion 100pts=$1, Stripe for cash donations), (4) tax receipt PDF generation using pdfkit with 501(c)(3) info, (5) bonus Foodie Points calculation (10% of donation), (6) charity partnership seeding (Feeding America affiliates, local food banks). Mobile: (7) ImpactScreen with CharityList, (8) donation flow (select charity, choose amount in points/cash, confirm, success + tax receipt), (9) personal impact dashboard (total donations, meals contributed, badges), (10) meals contributed estimation ($5 = 1 meal), (11) badge awards (Bronze 10 meals, Silver 50, Gold 100, Platinum 500), (12) donation timeline, (13) community stats display, (14) DonationLeaderboard (rank, username, meals donated), (15) food drives section with registration and QR check-in. Corporate: (16) admin dashboard for matching campaigns (2x, 3x multipliers), (17) matching notification in donation flow, (18) bonus points for matched amounts. Verification: (19) receipt photo upload for verified donations, (20) manual review workflow within 24 hours.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: High complexity (8) due to: dual payment processing (points + Stripe cash donations), tax receipt PDF generation requiring legal compliance (501(c)(3) info), corporate matching campaign system requiring admin dashboard, gamification with badges and leaderboards, food drive coordination with QR check-ins, verified donation system with photo upload and manual review workflow, charity partnership database seeding, and meals contributed estimation formula. Requires payment processing, PDF generation libraries, donation tracking compliance, review workflow implementation, and charitable giving domain knowledge. Lower priority but complex due to regulatory and payment considerations. Dependencies on Tasks 3, 4, & 11 for auth, points, and gamification.

complexityAnalysis.taskTitle: Setup Content Monetization and Creator Payouts
complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Structure as: Backend (1) Prisma schema extension (SocialPost earnings field, PromotionCampaign, CreatorEarnings models), (2) /api/v1/monetization endpoints (GET campaigns, POST enroll, GET earnings, POST payout), (3) earnings calculation engine (base $0.01/100 views + engagement bonuses: $0.005/like, $0.01/comment, $0.02/share), (4) promotion matching logic for higher rates, (5) earnings cap enforcement ($500/month standard, unlimited verified), (6) daily background job for earnings calculation (sum views/engagement since last run), (7) verified creator program implementation (eligibility: 1000+ followers, 10000+ views/30days, 90%+ engagement, no violations), (8) Stripe Connect integration for payouts, (9) weekly payout cron job (every Monday, min $10 balance), (10) payout support (direct deposit, PayPal, gift cards). Mobile: (11) EarningsScreen with dashboard (lifetime earnings, pending balance, payout history), (12) post earnings breakdown (top earning posts, views/engagement metrics), (13) active campaigns section for post enrollment, (14) payout settings with Stripe Connect onboarding flow, (15) verified creator badge on profile. Restaurant: (16) promotion campaign dashboard (web) with creation form (type, target item, budget, payout rate, duration, tags), (17) enrolled posts performance metrics, (18) post approval workflow (optional). (19) 'Sponsored' badge on enrolled posts, (20) content moderation penalty system (violations suspend earnings).
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Very high complexity (9) due to: Stripe Connect implementation for creator payouts requiring onboarding flows and tax compliance, sophisticated earnings calculation with multiple factors (views, likes, comments, shares, promotions), daily background job processing large volumes of engagement data, verified creator program with multi-factor eligibility checks, restaurant promotion campaign system requiring separate web dashboard, campaign matching algorithm, weekly payout automation with minimum balance checks, support for multiple payout methods, performance analytics for creators and restaurants, and content moderation integration affecting earnings. Requires payment infrastructure expertise, background job scalability, creator economy knowledge, and compliance considerations (1099 forms, tax reporting). Low priority but high complexity. Dependencies on Tasks 3, 9, & 10.

complexityAnalysis.taskTitle: Build Restaurant Detail Page with Maps Integration
complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Organize into: (1) RestaurantDetailScreen component creation, (2) restaurant header section (hero image, name, cuisine, rating, price range, open/closed status, distance, badges), (3) quick action buttons row (Directions, Call, Order Now, Save with animation, Share), (4) maps integration (react-native-maps installation), (5) DirectionsModal with map app options (Apple Maps, Google Maps, Waze deep links with fallback to web), (6) in-app map preview (MapView with restaurant pin and user location), (7) estimated travel time calculation (driving/walking/transit using Google Distance Matrix API), (8) information tabs implementation using react-native-tab-view (Overview, Menu, Reviews, Events), (9) location & address section (address with copy to clipboard, embedded map, 'Open in Maps', parking info), (10) points & loyalty info section (current balance, earning rate, active promotions, expiring points alert, available rewards, 'Connect Loyalty Account' CTA), (11) social proof section (recent Foodie posts, friends who visited, trending dishes, creator highlights), (12) backend /api/v1/restaurants/:id endpoint for full details (hours, location, menu, reviews, events, loyalty), (13) navigation integration from FeedScreen, (14) deep linking support for sharing restaurant URLs.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Moderate-high complexity (7) due to: react-native-maps integration with custom pins and user location, multiple map app deep linking (Apple Maps, Google Maps, Waze) with platform-specific URL schemes and app detection, Google Distance Matrix API for travel time estimation, tabbed interface with different content types (menu, reviews, events), clipboard API for address copying, sharing functionality, save animation, phone call linking, and backend endpoint aggregating data from multiple sources. Requires maps SDK expertise, deep linking knowledge, tab navigation implementation, and coordinate display of restaurant data from existing endpoints. High priority and dependencies on Task 8 for feed integration. Less complex than full-stack features but significant UI/UX and external API work.
